title: Enable clipboard paste (Ctrl+V) by clicking directly on each timeframe upload slot rectangle

requirements.summary: Make each TimeframeUploadSlot (M1, M3, M5) independently clickable to become the active paste target, so Ctrl+V pastes only into the selected slot with a visible focus indicator
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking on the M1 slot rectangle makes it the active paste target; Ctrl+V pastes only into the M1 slot.
Clicking on the M3 slot rectangle makes it the active paste target; Ctrl+V pastes only into the M3 slot.
Clicking on the M5 slot rectangle makes it the active paste target; Ctrl+V pastes only into the M5 slot.
Only one slot is active at a time — activating a new slot deactivates the previously active one.
The currently active slot displays a visible focus indicator (highlighted border or subtle glow).
Clicking outside all slots clears the active paste target so Ctrl+V does not accidentally paste into any slot.
All three slots continue to independently support file picker, camera capture, and drag-and-drop as before.
The 'Analisar Gráfico' button remains active only when at least one slot has an image.
All UI text remains in Portuguese (Brazil).

file_operations.description: Add clickable focus behavior to the upload slot rectangle: when the user clicks anywhere on the slot (except buttons), set it as the active paste target. Add an 'isActive' boolean prop from the parent, and apply a visible focus indicator (e.g., a blue ring or glow) when isActive is true. When the slot is active, attach a paste event listener to capture Ctrl+V and process the clipboard image only for this slot. Ensure existing file picker, drag-and-drop, and camera capture continue to work independently. Remove any global paste listener logic that was pasting into the first available slot. If the user selects a file or camera image for a different slot, emit a callback to the parent to deactivate this slot. Use Portuguese UI labels throughout.

Manage the single active paste target state across all three TimeframeUploadSlot instances (M1, M3, M5). Track which slot is currently active (e.g., activeSlotId: 'M1' | 'M3' | 'M5' | null). When a slot is clicked, set it as the active slot and deactivate the others. Pass an 'isActive' prop and an 'onActivate' callback to each TimeframeUploadSlot. Implement an 'onClick outside' handler (e.g., a click on the page background or the instruction card) that clears the active slot state so Ctrl+V does nothing when no slot is selected. Ensure the 'Analisar Gráfico' button logic remains unchanged (enabled only when at least one slot has an image). Verify that sessionStorage keys ('chartImage_M1', 'chartImage_M3', 'chartImage_M5') and navigation to ProcessingScreen are unaffected.
